issue: nth_digit(n, k) returns the k-th decimal digit of a non-negative integer n counted from the right, where k=1 is the ones digit, k=2 the tens digit, and so on. It is off by one: it returns the digit one place too far to the left. nth_digit(123, 1) returns 2 but should return 3 (the ones digit), nth_digit(123, 2) returns 1 but should return 2, and nth_digit(5, 1) returns 0 but should return 5. The bug is entirely in main.py, in the exponent used when dividing n down to the requested place.
